pi_items.source: Web search for Gideon v. Wainwright Supreme Court case (372 U.S. 335, 1963); setup_task.sh inserts this name into SQLite database (line 78); historical landmark case confirmed via uscourts.gov and Wikipedia

pi_items.metadata_value: The Sixth Amendment right to counsel
pi_items.verified_value: The Sixth Amendment right to counsel is a fundamental right applied to the states via the Fourteenth Amendment due process clause, and requires that indigent criminal defendants be provided counsel at trial.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 92-93 defines note_text_1 which contains this text; verified against web search confirming Sixth Amendment was made applicable to states; verifier.py line 59 checks for substring 'Sixth Amendment'

pi_items.verified_value: Overruled Betts v. Brady. This case incorporated the right to counsel to the states.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 103-104 defines note_text_2; web search confirms Gideon v. Wainwright reversed/overruled Betts v. Brady (1942); Wikipedia and Justia sources explicitly state 'The court reversed Betts' and 'overturning Betts'; verifier.py line 60 checks for exact string 'Overruled Betts v. Brady'

Task description states goal is to consolidate two notes into one; setup_task.sh creates two notes (lines 91-109); verifier.py line 73 checks that note_count equals 1; export_result.sh queries database for consolidated result

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic Gideon v. Wainwright case record created via SQLite database injection during task setup. All metadata claims are accurate: the case name 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is the correct landmark 1963 Supreme Court case; the required text about the Sixth Amendment right to counsel reflects the holding that the right applies to states via the Fourteenth Amendment; the claim that the case 'Overruled Betts v. Brady' is historically accurate as confirmed by authoritative sources (uscourts.gov, Wikipedia); the expected note count of 1 is the correct consolidation target. The task uses synthetic data injected directly into the Jurism application database, not real case files.
